agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Telemedicine platform enabling patients to book virtual consultations with licensed physicians. The platform must enforce medical licensing compliance, scheduling integrity, access control over protected health information (PHI), and auditability of clinical documentation.","bounded_context":"Appointment & Consultation Management — covers physician onboarding/verification, appointment scheduling, cancellation policies, medical record access control, and consultation note lifecycle.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Appointment","definition":"A scheduled time slot binding a patient to a licensed physician for a virtual consultation. Has a start time, end time, and lifecycle states (scheduled, in-progress, completed, cancelled)."},{"term":"Licensed Physician","definition":"A physician whose medical license number has been verified by the platform. Only licensed physicians may accept bookings."},{"term":"License Verification","definition":"The process of validating a physician's medical license number before they are eligible to appear in scheduling. A physician without a verified license cannot accept any bookings."},{"term":"Double-Booking","definition":"A scheduling conflict where two or more appointments overlap in time for the same physician. This is a forbidden state."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The 2-hour boundary before an appointment's start time. Within this window, only a Staff Administrator may cancel; outside it, the patient may also cancel."},{"term":"Staff Administrator","definition":"A platform role with elevated privileges, including the ability to cancel appointments inside the 2-hour cancellation window."},{"term":"Treating Physician","definition":"The specific physician assigned to a given appointment. Only this physician (not other physicians on the platform) may view the patient's medical records for that consultation."},{"term":"Medical Records","definition":"Protected health information belonging to a patient. Access is restricted to the patient themselves and their treating physician."},{"term":"Consultation Notes","definition":"Clinical documentation created by the treating physician during or after an appointment. These become read-only (locked) 48 hours after the appointment ends."},{"term":"Note Locking","definition":"The irreversible transition of consultation notes from editable to read-only state, triggered automatically 48 hours after the associated appointment's end time."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A patient may only book an appointment with a physician whose license has been verified.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing bookings with unlicensed physicians violates medical regulatory requirements and creates legal liability. The system is in a corrupt state if such a booking exists.","testable_assertion":"No appointment record may exist where the associated physician's license_verified status is false."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A physician cannot have two appointments whose time ranges overlap.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Double-booking is a physical constraint — a single physician cannot conduct two simultaneous consultations. If this state exists, data integrity is compromised.","testable_assertion":"For any physician, no two appointments may have overlapping [start_time, end_time) intervals."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A patient may cancel their appointment only if the current time is at least 2 hours before the appointment start time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 2-hour threshold is a business policy that could be adjusted (e.g., changed to 4 hours or 1 hour) without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"A patient-initiated cancellation request must be rejected if now() > appointment.start_time - 2 hours."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A Staff Administrator may cancel any appointment at any time, including within the 2-hour cancellation window.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a policy-level privilege assignment. The role's capabilities could be expanded or restricted without breaking system integrity.","testable_assertion":"A cancellation request from a Staff Administrator must succeed regardless of proximity to appointment start time."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"A physician's license number must be verified before the physician can accept any bookings.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a regulatory compliance gate. Bypassing it puts the platform in violation of medical practice laws. The system must enforce this as a hard precondition.","testable_assertion":"A physician with license_verified = false must not appear in available booking slots, and any direct booking attempt must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Patient medical records are visible only to the patient themselves and the treating physician for a given consultation.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"This is a PHI access control rule rooted in healthcare privacy regulations (e.g., HIPAA). Violation constitutes a data breach.","testable_assertion":"Any access request for a patient's medical records must be denied unless the requester is the patient OR the physician assigned to an active/completed appointment with that patient."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Consultation notes become locked (read-only) 48 hours after the appointment ends.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 48-hour window is a business policy for clinical documentation. It could be changed to 24 or 72 hours without corrupting data integrity, though the locking mechanism itself is important.","testable_assertion":"Any edit attempt on consultation notes where now() > appointment.end_time + 48 hours must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"Once consultation notes are locked, they cannot be unlocked or modified.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Locked clinical documentation serves as a legal medical record. Allowing modification after locking would compromise auditability and legal defensibility.","testable_assertion":"No write operation may succeed on consultation notes whose status is 'locked', regardless of the requester's role."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A physician with a verified license and available time slots","when":"A patient attempts to book an appointment within an open slot","then":"The appointment is created successfully, the slot is reserved, and no overlapping booking can be made for that physician during the same time range"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A physician whose license has NOT been verified","when":"A patient attempts to book an appointment with that physician","then":"The booking is rejected with a clear error indicating the physician is not available for appointments"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A physician with an existing appointment from 10:00 to 10:30","when":"Another patient attempts to book an appointment with the same physician from 10:15 to 10:45","then":"The booking is rejected due to a scheduling conflict (double-booking prevention)"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A patient with a scheduled appointment starting in 3 hours","when":"The patient requests cancellation","then":"The cancellation succeeds and the physician's time slot is released"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A patient with a scheduled appointment starting in 1 hour","when":"The patient requests cancellation","then":"The cancellation is rejected with a message indicating only a staff administrator can cancel within 2 hours of the start time"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A scheduled appointment starting in 30 minutes","when":"A Staff Administrator requests cancellation","then":"The cancellation succeeds regardless of the cancellation window"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A completed appointment between Patient A and Physician X","when":"Physician Y (not the treating physician) requests access to Patient A's medical records","then":"Access is denied"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A completed appointment between Patient A and Physician X","when":"Physician X requests access to Patient A's medical records","then":"Access is granted"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A completed appointment that ended 47 hours ago with editable consultation notes","when":"The treating physician edits the consultation notes","then":"The edit is saved successfully"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A completed appointment that ended 49 hours ago","when":"The treating physician attempts to edit the consultation notes","then":"The edit is rejected because the notes are now locked (read-only)"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"Consultation notes that have been locked","when":"Any user (including Staff Administrator) attempts to modify them","then":"The modification is rejected; locked notes are immutable"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Physician's license is revoked after appointments are already booked","expected_behavior":"Existing future appointments should be flagged or cancelled. The physician must be immediately removed from the bookable pool. Completed past appointments remain valid as the physician was licensed at the time of consultation."},{"scenario":"Patient attempts to book an appointment at the exact boundary of an existing appointment (e.g., new appointment starts at the exact end time of an existing one)","expected_behavior":"This should succeed — back-to-back appointments are allowed as long as time ranges do not overlap. The interval comparison should be [start, end) — exclusive of the end time."},{"scenario":"Cancellation request arrives at exactly the 2-hour boundary","expected_behavior":"The system must define whether 'exactly 2 hours before' is within or outside the window. Recommendation: treat exactly 2 hours as patient-cancellable (inclusive boundary)."},{"scenario":"Consultation notes are being actively edited when the 48-hour locking deadline arrives","expected_behavior":"The in-progress edit should be rejected upon save. The locking mechanism should take precedence. The system should warn the physician as the deadline approaches."},{"scenario":"A patient has multiple appointments with different physicians","expected_behavior":"Each treating physician sees only the medical records relevant to their consultation. Access does not extend across all of the patient's records beyond the treatment relationship."},{"scenario":"Appointment is cancelled — can the physician still see medical records?","expected_behavior":"Access should be revoked for cancelled appointments. The treating relationship is voided upon cancellation."},{"scenario":"Staff Administrator attempts to modify locked consultation notes","expected_behavior":"Modification is denied. Note immutability applies universally, regardless of role. If a correction is legally required, an addendum mechanism should be used instead."},{"scenario":"System clock discrepancy or timezone mismatch affects cancellation window or note locking","expected_behavior":"All time comparisons must use a canonical timezone (UTC) stored at the system level. Client-side timezone must not influence business rule enforcement."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Non-compliance with medical licensing rules or PHI access controls exposes the platform to regulatory fines, malpractice liability, and potential shutdown. Double-booking wastes physician time and may require refunds. The 48-hour note-locking policy supports defensibility in malpractice disputes.","operational":"License verification is a critical onboarding gate — delays in verification directly reduce physician supply. The cancellation window policy balances patient flexibility against physician schedule stability. Note locking reduces support burden around documentation disputes.","user":"Patients trust the platform to connect them only with verified physicians and to protect their medical records. Physicians trust that their schedules are conflict-free and that they have adequate time to finalize documentation. Staff administrators need clear authority for exception handling.","risk":"PHI access violations carry HIPAA-level regulatory risk. Unlicensed physician bookings create malpractice exposure. Mutable locked notes undermine legal defensibility of clinical records. Double-booking erodes physician trust and platform reliability.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Patients","impact":"Depend on license verification for safety, access controls for privacy, and cancellation policy for scheduling flexibility."},{"group":"Physicians","impact":"Depend on double-booking prevention for schedule integrity, 48-hour edit window for documentation accuracy, and access controls to limit liability exposure."},{"group":"Staff Administrators","impact":"Serve as the exception-handling layer for cancellations inside the 2-hour window. Need clear authority boundaries."},{"group":"Compliance / Legal Team","impact":"Rely on license verification, PHI access controls, and note immutability to maintain regulatory compliance and legal defensibility."},{"group":"Platform Operator","impact":"Bears liability for compliance failures. Revenue depends on physician supply (gated by verification) and patient trust (gated by privacy controls)."}]},"confidence":{"level":82,"reasoning":"The specification is clear, internally consistent, and covers scheduling, access control, cancellation policy, and documentation lifecycle without contradiction. Confidence is not higher because the spec does not address several operationally important details: the scope of medical record access (per-appointment vs. per-patient), license revocation handling, and whether the 2-hour boundary is inclusive or exclusive.","high_confidence_areas":["Double-booking prevention is an unambiguous invariant","License verification as a booking precondition is clearly stated","Cancellation policy with role-based authority is well-defined","Note locking at 48 hours is a clear temporal rule","PHI access restricted to patient and treating physician"],"low_confidence_areas":["Scope of 'treating physician' access — per-appointment or ongoing after relationship ends?","Behavior when a physician's license is revoked mid-tenure with existing bookings","Whether the 2-hour cancellation boundary is inclusive or exclusive","Whether locked notes can receive addenda or corrections via a separate mechanism","Timezone and clock handling for time-sensitive business rules"],"assumptions":["The 48-hour note-locking deadline is based on appointment end time, not creation time of the notes","Back-to-back appointments (non-overlapping, sharing a boundary) are permitted","A cancelled appointment revokes the treating physician's access to the patient's medical records","Note locking is irreversible — no unlock mechanism exists","Staff Administrator cancellation authority has no additional constraints beyond role membership"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Telemedicine platform enabling patients to book virtual consultations with licensed physicians. The platform must enforce medical licensing compliance, scheduling integrity, access control over protected health information (PHI), and auditability of clinical documentation.","bounded_context":"Appointment & Consultation Management — covers physician onboarding/verification, appointment scheduling, cancellation policies, medical record access control, and co

content.thinking:
Analyzing a telemedicine platform domain with clear rules from product and compliance teams.

Let me break down the rules:
1. Patient can only book with a licensed physician
2. No double-booking physicians (overlapping appointments)
3. Cancellation: patient can cancel up to 2hrs before; after that only staff admin
4. Physician license must be verified before accepting bookings
5. Medical records visible only to treating physician and patient
6. Consultation notes locked (read-only) 48hrs after appointment ends

These rules are clear and well-defined. No contradictions. Let me classify: no escalation needed, type would be "none" but the schema requires enum values. 

Actually, escalations can be an empty array, so I don't need to force a type value. Now I'll produce the full domain analysis.
Telemedicine platform domain analysis. Clear rules, no contradictions. Let me produce the full analysis.
